mapping_metadata.analyzer: Senior Test Automation Mapping Specialist Agent

test_case_mappings.test_case_description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card display and behavior on Print and Payment History page when a subscription plan is paused, including verification of complimentary pages progress bar, additional pages tracking, tooltips, and page usage information display across different printing scenarios.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Orchestrates the complete flow of creating and claiming a virtual printer plus adding shipping address. Calls get_org_aware_token() to obtain org token and tenant ID. Calls create_virtual_printer() to create the printer. Unpacks the returned PrinterData tuple. Calls claim_virtual_printer() with all required parameters. Calls add_address_to_tenant() with org token and tenant ID. Sleeps 600 seconds as temporary fix for backend synchronization issues. Returns the PrinterData object.
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This method creates and claims a virtual printer which is the first step in enrolling a printer. After claiming, the printer can be enrolled in an Instant Ink plan. This fulfills the printer setup portion of precondition 1.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Retrieves plans filtered by a specific attribute value. Calls get_plans_data() to get all plans. Converts value to integer. Filters plans array to find plans where plan[key] equals value. Returns the list of matching plan objects. This provides filtered plan selection for test data.
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This method retrieves plan data for a specific page count (50 pages or higher as required by precondition 1). It can be used to get the plan details needed for enrollment.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Retrieves all Instant Ink plans data from Agena API for the current country. Constructs URL to Agena plans endpoint with country, program=i_ink, and obsolete=false query parameters. Calls get_token_gemini_client() to get service token. Sends GET request with headers. Returns the response data (array of plan objects).
components_found_in_this_batch.usage_example: plans = get_plans_data(); plan_50 = [p for p in plans if p['pages'] >= 50][0]
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This method retrieves all available plans for the test country, which can be filtered to find plans with 50 pages or higher as required by precondition 1.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Contains printer SKU profiles organized by printer model codename and geographic region. Each printer profile includes SKU numbers, easy enrollment capability flags, and supply variant types (IIC or IPH) for different regions (NA, EU, APJ). Used by test flows to select appropriate printer SKUs for virtual printer creation.
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This data file provides printer model and SKU information needed to create a virtual printer for enrollment. The printer profile determines which SKU to use when calling create_virtual_printer().
Default first name used during account creation which is part of the enrollment precondition setup.
Default last name used during account creation which is part of the enrollment precondition setup.
Default password used during account creation which is part of the enrollment precondition setup.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Retrieves subscription data from Gemini API using service token. Calls get_token_gemini_client() to get Gemini service token. Constructs URL to instantink /api/users/subscriptions/{id} endpoint. Sends GET request with headers. Returns the response data dictionary containing full subscription details.
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This method retrieves complete subscription data from the API, which can be used to verify that the subscription has no free months information. The returned data can be inspected for free months fields.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Extracts subscription state from Gemini subscription data. Calls subscription_data_from_gemini() with the subscription ID. Attempts to navigate the nested dictionary path userSubInfo -> subscriptionInfo -> minSubInfo -> subscriptionState. Returns the subscriptionState value if found, None if path doesn't exist.
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: While this method specifically extracts state, it uses subscription_data_from_gemini() which returns full subscription data that can be checked for free months information.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Extracts subscription state from Gemini subscription data. Calls subscription_data_from_gemini() with the subscription ID. Navigates the nested dictionary path to extract subscriptionState. Returns the state value or None.
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This method directly retrieves the subscription status, which can be used to verify the subscription is in 'subscribed' status as required by precondition 2.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Validates that subscription reaches expected state within 60 seconds. Records start time. Enters loop that runs for up to 60 seconds. Calls get_subscription_state() with subscription ID. If state matches expected_state, logs info message and breaks. Sleeps 1 second between checks. Asserts state equals expected_state after loop.
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This method validates that the subscription is in the expected 'SUBSCRIBED' status with polling and timeout, which is exactly what precondition 2 requires.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Retrieves complete subscription data from Gemini API including subscription state and all other subscription details.
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This method retrieves full subscription data which includes the subscription status that needs to be verified for precondition 2.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.
precondition_mapping.notes: No UI page objects, locators, or methods for clicking pause plan buttons or links were found in this batch. This batch contains test data files and helper functions but no page objects for the Instant Ink dashboard or CIT modal. The pause plan UI interaction components likely exist in other batches containing page object files for the dashboard or subscription management pages.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Orchestrates the complete flow of creating and claiming a virtual printer plus adding shipping address. This is a high-level setup function that combines printer creation, claiming, and address setup into a single operation.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This method sets up a virtual printer which is required for precondition 1 (enrolling a printer with a plan). It creates the printer, claims it to the user account, and adds the shipping address needed for enrollment.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Creates a virtual printer through the G2 simulator API with retry logic for each step. Returns PrinterData tuple with entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, cloud_id, postcard, and fingerprint.
matched_methods.usage_example: printer = create_virtual_printer(); serial = printer.entity_id; model = printer.model_number
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This method creates a virtual printer which is the first step in fulfilling precondition 1 (printer enrollment). The created printer can then be enrolled in an Instant Ink plan.

matched_methods.method_signature: def claim_virtual_printer(org_token, tenant_id, model_number, device_uuid, postcard, fingerprint)
matched_methods.what_it_does: Claims a virtual printer to a tenant account through the UCDE API with retry logic. Associates the printer with the user's account so it can be enrolled in Instant Ink.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This method claims the virtual printer to the user's account, which is necessary before the printer can be enrolled in an Instant Ink plan (precondition 1).

matched_methods.what_it_does: Adds a shipping address to a tenant account via the Gemini comfe API. The address is required for subscription enrollment and cartridge shipments.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This method adds the shipping address required for Instant Ink enrollment, which is part of the printer enrollment process in precondition 1.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Retrieves all Instant Ink plans data from Agena API for the current country. Returns array of plan objects with details like pages, pricing, plan IDs.
matched_methods.usage_example: plans = get_plans_data(); plan_50 = [p for p in plans if p['pages'] >= 50][0]
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This method retrieves plan data which is needed to select a plan with 50 pages or higher for enrollment as required by precondition 1.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Retrieves plans filtered by a specific attribute value. Filters the plans array to find plans matching the specified criteria.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This method provides filtered plan selection to find plans with 50 pages or higher as required by precondition 1.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Retrieves complete subscription data from Gemini API using service token. Returns full subscription details including state, free months, plan info, billing info.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This method retrieves full subscription data which can be used to verify precondition 1 (no free months) and precondition 2 (subscribed status).

matched_methods.what_it_does: Extracts subscription state from Gemini subscription data. Returns the subscriptionState value or None if not found.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This method retrieves the subscription status which is needed to verify precondition 2 (subscription is in 'subscribed' status).

matched_methods.what_it_does: Validates that subscription reaches expected state within 60 seconds with polling. Asserts state equals expected_state.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This method validates the subscription is in 'SUBSCRIBED' status as required by precondition 2, with automatic polling and timeout.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Sends an RTP (Real-Time Printing) device status message to the G2 simulator for a virtual printer. Constructs complex binary telemetry data with page counts, supply levels, and printer state. This simulates printer reporting which triggers backend processing for page tracking and billing.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This method simulates printer telemetry reporting which is needed for steps 10 and 12 where pages are printed. The RTP device status updates the backend with page counts which then appear in the UI.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Returns the list of ink colors for the current printer based on supply variant. Returns ['CMY', 'K'] for IPH or ['K', 'C', 'M', 'Y'] for IIC.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This method provides the correct color array for the printer's supply variant, which is needed when constructing RTP payloads and for supply level reporting.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Parses date strings in multiple formats into datetime objects. Handles various international date formats including US, European, and ISO formats.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This utility method can be used to parse date strings from the UI if date validation is needed, though the current test case doesn't explicitly require date parsing.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Extracts and parses a date from text containing mixed content. Uses regex patterns to find dates in various formats within text strings.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This utility method can extract dates from UI text if date validation is needed in the billing cycle card.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Obtains an organization-aware user access token through a multi-step token exchange process. Returns tuple of (org_token, tenant_id).
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This method obtains the authentication token needed for API calls during printer setup and enrollment (precondition 1).

matched_methods.what_it_does: Obtains an OAuth access token for the Gemini client using client credentials flow. Returns the access token string.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This method obtains service-level authentication token needed for API calls to retrieve plans, subscription data, and perform other backend operations during precondition setup.

matched_methods.method_signature: def retry_operation(operation, operation_name='Operation', max_attempts=5, delay=2, on_retry=None)
matched_methods.what_it_does: Generic retry wrapper that executes an operation with configurable retry logic. Provides automatic retry with delay and optional callback for cleanup/recovery.
matched_methods.usage_example: result = retry_operation(lambda: api_call(), operation_name='API Call', max_attempts=3, delay=5)
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This utility method provides retry logic that can be used to wrap any flaky operations in the test flow, improving test reliability.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Convenience setup function that calls set_globals with configuration values extracted from GlobalState. Initializes all module-level globals from GlobalState configuration.
matched_methods.usage_example: setup()  # Called at test initialization to set up environment from GlobalState
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This method initializes the test environment configuration which is required before any test operations can be performed. It sets up all global variables needed by other helper functions.

matched_helpers.what_it_does: Test data file containing comprehensive country and locale mapping configuration. Maps country names to ISO codes, supported languages, geographic regions (NA, EU, APJ), and card payment gateway assignments (PGS or 2CO). Supports 38 countries across three major regions.
matched_helpers.usage_example: locale_data = load_json_file('test_flows_common/locale_map.json'); us_config = locale_data['United States']; country_code = us_config['code']; region = us_config['region']
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: This data file provides country and locale configuration needed for test setup. The country code and region information is used throughout the test flow to configure API calls and determine which features are available.

matched_helpers.what_it_does: Test data file containing printer SKU profiles organized by printer model codename and geographic region. Each printer profile includes SKU numbers, easy enrollment capability flags, and supply variant types (IIC or IPH) for different regions.
matched_helpers.usage_example: printer_profiles = load_json_file('test_flows_common/printer_profiles.json'); manhattan_skus = printer_profiles['manhattan_yeti']['skus']['NA']; sku = manhattan_skus[0]['sku']; supply_variant = manhattan_skus[0]['supplyvariant']
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: This data file provides printer model and SKU information needed to create a virtual printer for enrollment. The supply variant (IIC or IPH) is critical for determining the correct RTP payload format and color array.

matched_helpers.what_it_does: Test data file containing Instant Ink subscription offer configurations organized by country code. Each offer includes type (free or prepaid), unique GUID identifier, offer identifier code, payment requirement flag, and pricing in cents.
matched_helpers.usage_example: offers = load_json_file('test_flows_common/offers.json'); us_offers = offers['US']; prepaid_offers = [o for o in us_offers if o['type'] == 'prepaid']
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: This data file provides offer configurations that can be used during enrollment. While the test case doesn't explicitly mention offers, enrollment typically requires selecting an offer.

matched_helpers.what_it_does: Test data file containing payment method credentials and configuration for automated payment testing. Includes test credit card numbers for major card networks, IBAN details for direct debit, PayPal test account credentials, and Google Pay test credentials.
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: This data file provides payment method test credentials that may be needed during enrollment if the plan requires payment setup.

matched_constants.relevance_reason: Default first name used during account creation which is part of the enrollment precondition setup.
Default last name used during account creation which is part of the enrollment precondition setup.
Default password used during account creation which is part of the enrollment precondition setup.

matched_constants.constant_value: Large nested dictionary with configuration for pie, stage, and production stacks
matched_constants.relevance_reason: This configuration dictionary contains all environment-specific endpoint URLs needed for API calls throughout the test flow including instantink_url, agena_url, and other service endpoints.
This constant contains the loaded offers data which can be used for offer selection during enrollment.
Gmail API client ID used for email verification code retrieval during account creation.
Gmail API client secret used for email verification code retrieval during account creation.
Gmail API refresh token used for email verification code retrieval during account creation.
